confirmed.title: TableCard widget renders boolean cells as raw 'true'/'false' instead of a localized label
confirmed.detail: In formatCell(), the branch `if (typeof value === 'string' || typeof value === 'boolean') return String(value);` returns the English JS literals 'true'/'false' for boolean column values. The main table cell renderer (packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:142-143) localizes the exact same case via t('table.boolean.true_label')/t('table.boolean.false_label') so a pt-BR viewer sees 'sim'/'não'. TableCard (a dashboard table-widget) was given locale-aware number grouping in a prior round (numberFormatter at line 46/108-109) but the boolean path was missed, leaving a locale-insensitive, untranslated truth label. Repro: configure a dashboard TableCard widget whose column data includes a boolean field (e.g. `active: true`); under any non-English panel locale the cell still renders the literal string 'true'/'false' rather than the localized table.boolean.* label used everywhere else. Fix: thread the translator into formatCell and return t('table.boolean.true_label','true') / t('table.boolean.false_label','false') for the boolean case (mirroring cells.tsx), keeping String(value) only for genuine strings.
confirmed.why_real:
Code confirmed: packages-js/ui/src/widgets/TableCard.tsx:111-112, formatCell() returns `String(value)` for the `typeof value === 'boolean'` branch, so a boolean column value renders the literal English 'true'/'false' verbatim to all users regardless of panel locale. This is genuine, visible, untranslated user-facing UI text in a first-party (non-vendored) dashboard table-widget.

Novelty vs ledger: the ledger contains a Round-9 entry for this exact file/function ("Dashboard TableCard formatCell uses String(value) for numbers — no locale grouping", line 104) but that fix only addressed the NUMBER branch (numberFormatter), explicitly leaving the boolean branch as String(value). A Round-4 entry covers cells.tsx BooleanCell aria-label — a different file/component. No ledger entry covers the boolean branch of TableCard's formatCell. So this is not a duplicate of R1-R28.

Minor inaccuracy in the finding (does not negate it): it claims the main table renderer (cells.tsx BooleanCell) makes a pt-BR viewer "see 'sim'/'não'". In reality BooleanCell renders a language-neutral glyph (✓/—) and localizes only the aria-label, not visible text. So TableCard is not literally failing to mirror visible 'sim'/'não' output. Nonetheless the underlying defect — TableCard visibly emitting untranslated English 'true'/'false' — is real and reproduces: configure a dashboard TableCard whose column data includes a boolean field; under any locale the cell shows literal 'true'/'false'. The suggested fix (thread translator, return t('table.boolean.true_label')/t('table.boolean.false_label')) is a valid remediation.

Confidence high on is_real and is_duplicate=false; the only caveat is the finding's slight mischaracterization of the comparison renderer, which affects the rationale but not the existence of the defect.
